fundamentals.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Micron Technology, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, and sells memory and storage products in the United States, Taiwan, Japan, Mainland China, Hong Kong, Europe, and internationally. It operates through the Cloud Memory Business Unit; Core Data Center Business Unit; Mobile and Client Business Unit; and Automotive and Embedded Business Unit segments. The company provides memory products, including dynamic random access memory components and modules, CXL-based memory, LPDDR components and modules, graphics memory, high-bandwidth memory, and data center memory products; multichip packages (MCP) comprising embedded multimedia card-based, universal flash storage-based, and NAND-based MCPs; and technology leadership products that include 1y DRAM and G9 NAND technologies. It also offers storage products, such as data center solid-state drives (SSD), client SSD storage, and auto and industrial SSD storage; managed NAND; NAND flash; NOR flash; and memory cards. In addition, the company provides design tools, including FBGA and part decoders; DRAM power calculators; NAND power calculators; simulation models; chipset compatibility guides; serial presence-detection tools; cross-reference tools; UFSparm; SSD firmware; software and drivers; storage executive software; and obsolete part catalogs. It markets its semiconductor memory and storage products under the Micron and Crucial brands. The company serves the data center, PC, graphics, networking, automotive, industrial, and consumer embedded markets, as well as the smartphone and other mobile-device markets. It sells its products through its direct sales force, independent sales representatives, distributors, and retailers; web-based customer direct sales channel; and channel and distribution partners. Micron Technology, Inc. was founded in 1978 and is headquartered in Boise, Idaho.

judgment.summary: Micron exhibits exceptional growth and profitability, though its valuation metrics warrant careful consideration.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 33.2%; next earnings date is 2026-06-24.
positives: Revenue growth 196.3% YoY and Earnings growth 756.0% YoY indicate explosive expansion.
Profit margin 41.5% and Operating margin 67.6% demonstrate superior operational efficiency.
Forward P/E of 8.64 is exceptionally low relative to its growth and trailing P/E of 42.83.
PEG ratio of 0.31 suggests significant undervaluation relative to expected earnings growth.
Return on equity of 39.8% and Return on assets of 20.1% show strong capital utilization.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 14.90 is high, indicating significant leverage.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 17.59 is elevated, suggesting the market values its revenue highly.
Mean analyst target of $652.98 is considerably below the current spot price of $905.92.
Beta of 1.92 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Micron is experiencing an unprecedented AI boom, with recent analyst reports highlighting its strong position in the memory trade and suggesting significant upside potential. Articles compare Micron favorably against competitors like Intel, with several analysts forecasting substantial rallies and consensus ratings leaning towards strong buy. The company's recent earnings beat and strong growth estimates further bolster the bullish narrative.
